title: Clustering alignment for population structure analysis
summary: Developed methods and tools for aligning latent ancestries across repeated population structure analyses so results remain interpretable across runs and choices of K.
details: Created Clumppling for cluster alignment, mode consolidation, and visualization of correspondence across inferred population structures. | Built workflow support for end-to-end population structure analysis and downstream interactive visualization.

title: Alignment-aware single-cell clustering analysis
summary: Extending clustering alignment ideas to the single-cell analysis pipeline to compare models, identify cluster-informative genes, and stabilize downstream interpretation.
details: Developing measures of clustering consistency for robust model comparison. | Designing feature-level interpretation metrics to surface informative genes.

title: ML-based methods for multivariate genetic association analysis
summary: Designed ML-MAGES, a machine-learning framework for shrinkage, pattern discovery, and gene-level aggregation in high-dimensional genetic association studies.
details: Uses neural-network-based supervised learning to account for LD-induced inflation. | Combines effect size shrinkage with flexible clustering of multi-trait association patterns.

title: Mathematical properties of allele-sharing dissimilarities
summary: Analyzing within- and between-population dissimilarity measures as functions of allele-frequency distributions to clarify when and how these statistics should be used.
details: Derives mathematical constraints and interpretable bounds. | Connects theory to empirical population-genetic settings.

title: Cultural evolution through population-genetic methods
summary: Adapts ideas from genetic evolution to study how cultural traits, social learning, and inherited conventions vary across groups and over time.
details: Uses network-based clustering to recover hierarchical structure in cultural variation. | Studies cultural transmission processes such as surnaming decisions and intergenerational inheritance.
